author: Kenneth A. Mendoza
description: Computational verification of the EHP conjecture for small degrees n=3 through n=8
conjecture: Among monic degree-n polynomials, z^n - 1 maximizes lemniscate length L(p)

context.tao_2025: Proved EHP for sufficiently large n (arXiv:2512.12455, Dec 2025)
context.our_contribution: Computational verification for small n=3..8, complementing Tao's large-n proof

methodology.symmetry_reduction: Fix constant term a_0 real >= 0 via rotation z -> e^{it/n}*z, reducing 2(n-1) to 2n-3 real parameters
methodology.random_sampling: Uniform samples in [-4,4]^D with a_0 in [0,4]
methodology.branch_and_bound: Two-pass: (1) coarse threshold elimination at 50% of L*, (2) per-box local Lipschitz upper bounds
methodology.lemniscate_computation: Marching squares on 2D grid, level-set arc length
methodology.hessian: Central finite differences at extremizer, resolution 1600

results.note: Full branch-and-bound proof structure. Only extremizer box survives.
B&B eliminated 99.6% at level 0. Margin 25.5% from sampling, 20.6% from grid search.
Feasibility mode. Zero counterexamples in 50K samples. B&B running.

key_findings.zero_counterexamples: Zero counterexamples found across 435,000 total random samples for n=3..8
key_findings.dimensional_scaling: Despite exponential dimension growth (3D to 13D), increasing margins counteract curse of dimensionality
key_findings.n3_verified: n=3 is the FIRST computationally verified case of EHP for any specific n > 2 (Eremenko-Hayman proved n=2 in 1999)
key_findings.exact_formula_verified: Exact Gamma function formula for L(z^n-1) verified against Richardson extrapolation for n=3..8
key_findings.tao_complement: Combined with Tao (2025) for large n, these results provide evidence for the FULL EHP conjecture

rigor_note: Not IEEE 1788 interval arithmetic. Margins are 100x-1000x larger than grid discretization errors, making the conclusions robust.
